text.title: Trade-off Decision Methods Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the trade-off-decision-methods methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale; the final rule is the skip-this-methodology gate referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: Weighted decision matrix MUST be the default method. Other methods MUST be chosen only when matrix limitations are documented (multi-attribute conflicts, deep uncertainty, time-driven payoff, strategic positioning).
rule.rationale: Matrix is the cheapest defensible method; defaulting elsewhere produces ceremony without payoff.

rule.statement: ATAM MUST be selected when the decision involves > 2 quality attributes with documented conflict (perf vs sec vs cost), or when stakeholder breadth is cross-org and audit-defensibility matters.
rule.rationale: ATAM produces utility trees + scenarios + tradeoffs that a flat matrix cannot capture.

rule.statement: Cost of Delay MUST be applied when payoff is time-sensitive (market window, regulatory deadline, dependency chain). The decision MUST quantify $/week of delay.

rationale.source: Reinertsen — Principles of Product Development Flow.

rule.rationale: Time-driven decisions are systematically deprioritised when only weighted matrix is used; CoD surfaces hidden cost.

rule.statement: Wardley Mapping MUST be applied when the decision spans strategic positioning, build-vs-buy of evolving capabilities, or commodity-vs-custom routing.
rule.rationale: Wardley maps reveal evolution-stage mismatches that matrix scoring misses.

rule.statement: Real Options framing MUST be applied when uncertainty is high, the decision is reversible-with-cost, and waiting carries documented value (information arrival). Decision MUST quantify the option value vs commit-now value.
rule.rationale: High-uncertainty decisions optimised by weighted matrix often commit prematurely; Real Options models the value of waiting.

rule.statement: When prerequisites hold (question, options, uncertainty, breadth), apply r1 → r5 and emit the ADR.
Do NOT use when (a) single option, (b) routine reversible choice, (c) hard mandate. Exit with note.
rule.rationale: Method-routing ceremony for trivial decisions wastes review.
